title: Understanding Gravity: The Force that Shapes Our Universe
summary: Gravity is a fundamental force of nature, described by Sir Isaac Newton's laws and later refined through Einstein's theory of general relativity. It governs the motion of planets, stars, and galaxies.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: Gravity is an attractive force that exists between any two masses. It's the reason we stay anchored to Earth and why celestial bodies are bound in their orbits.

sections.heading: How it works
sections.body: Gravity results from the curvature of spacetime caused by an object's mass. The more massive an object, the stronger its gravitational pull.
key_points: Massive objects curve space and time

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.body: Gravity shapes the cosmos, dictates planetary systems' dynamics, influences climate by affecting ocean currents on Earth.
key_points: Forms galaxies and holds them together

key_facts: Gravity is proportional to mass but inversely proportional to the square of distance between objects
